name: preconditions_unsatisfiable
code:
proof fn phi_1_preconditions_unsatisfiable(pm: &ProcessManager, container_ptr: ContainerPtr, cpu_id: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        0 <= cpu_id < NUM_CPUS,
        pm.container_dom().contains(container_ptr),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).owned_cpus@.contains(cpu_id),
        pm.get_cpu(cpu_id).current_thread.is_None(),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).depth != 0,
        pm.root_container != container_ptr,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If provable, the preconditions are contradictory and transfer_idle_cpu can never be called.

name: self_parent_loop
code:
proof fn phi_2_self_parent_loop(pm: &ProcessManager, container_ptr: ContainerPtr, cpu_id: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        0 <= cpu_id < NUM_CPUS,
        pm.container_dom().contains(container_ptr),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).owned_cpus@.contains(cpu_id),
        pm.get_cpu(cpu_id).current_thread.is_None(),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).depth != 0,
        pm.root_container != container_ptr,
    ensures
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).parent == Some(container_ptr),
{
}
reason: If provable, a container can be its own parent, making the CPU transfer a no-op and breaking the tree structure.

name: parent_not_in_domain
code:
proof fn phi_3_parent_not_in_domain(pm: &ProcessManager, container_ptr: ContainerPtr, cpu_id: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        0 <= cpu_id < NUM_CPUS,
        pm.container_dom().contains(container_ptr),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).owned_cpus@.contains(cpu_id),
        pm.get_cpu(cpu_id).current_thread.is_None(),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).depth != 0,
        pm.root_container != container_ptr,
    ensures
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).parent.is_Some()
            && !pm.container_dom().contains(pm.get_container(container_ptr).parent.unwrap()),
{
}
reason: If provable, the parent container is not in the domain, so inserting the CPU into the parent would access invalid memory.

name: depth_exceeds_container_count
code:
proof fn phi_4_depth_exceeds_container_count(pm: &ProcessManager, container_ptr: ContainerPtr, cpu_id: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        0 <= cpu_id < NUM_CPUS,
        pm.container_dom().contains(container_ptr),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).owned_cpus@.contains(cpu_id),
        pm.get_cpu(cpu_id).current_thread.is_None(),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).depth != 0,
        pm.root_container != container_ptr,
    ensures
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).depth as int > pm.container_dom().len(),
{
}
reason: If provable, tree depth exceeds the number of containers, indicating a malformed container tree invariant.

name: cpu_owner_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_5_cpu_owner_mismatch(pm: &ProcessManager, container_ptr: ContainerPtr, cpu_id: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        0 <= cpu_id < NUM_CPUS,
        pm.container_dom().contains(container_ptr),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).owned_cpus@.contains(cpu_id),
        pm.get_cpu(cpu_id).current_thread.is_None(),
        pm.get_container(container_ptr).depth != 0,
        pm.root_container != container_ptr,
    ensures
        pm.get_cpu(cpu_id).owning_container != container_ptr,
{
}
reason: If provable, cpu_list and container owned_cpus are inconsistent—the CPU is listed under a container that the cpu_list disagrees with.
